case_title: MAY CHEN et al RESPONDENT VS. REBECCA TOVSHTEYN APPELLANT

docket_entries.description: STIPULATION TO USE JUDGE PRO TEM AND ORDER

RECORD ON APPEAL COURT JUDGMENT-SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL FILED BY APPELLANT TOVSHTEYN, REBECCA AS TO RESPONDENT CHEN, MAY CHEN, ZI QUANG HEARING SET FOR OCT-04-2007 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 525 SMALL CLAIMS CASE NUMBER 822076
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF HEARING ON APPEAL
RECORD REMANDED TO SMALL CLAIMS (SEE SMALL CLAIMS #822076; NO EXHIBITS RETURNED TO SMALL CLAIMS DIVISION)
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-04-2007 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 525
SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL CALENDAR OF OCT-04-2007 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF OCT-4-07 BY JUDGE PRO TEM ELLIOTT A. MYLES, CLERK-BETH WILBER, NOTHING ON RECORD.

NOTICE OF LIMITED SCOPE REPRESENTATION, U.D. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE (ASSIGNED TO REPRESENT RESPONDENTS, MAY CHEN AND ZI QIANG CHEN, AT SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL - CODE USED DUE TO PROBLEMS WITH IJS IN ADDING ATTORNEY NAME.) FILED BY RESPONDENT CHEN, MAY CHEN, ZI QUANG SMITH, ZACHARY ASSIGNED TO REPRESENT PARTY FOR U. D. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT RESPONDENT CHEN, ZI QUANG RECOVER FROM APPELLANT TOVSHTEYN, REBECCA $525.00 PRINCIPAL, $60.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $585.00, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE APPEAL WAS NOT A BAD FAITH APPEAL UNDER CCP 116.790; AND THAT THE TEST OF "NECESSARY TO ACHIEVE SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE" IN CCP 116.780 WAS NOT MET. ZI QIANG CHEN AS A WITNESS, AND THE OWNER, COULD HAVE PRESENTED THIS CASE WITHOUT MAY CHEN. (SEE SCANNED DOC) NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND CERTIFICATE OF MAILING FILED

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
MINI MINUTES: Judge Pro Tem Elliott A. Myles, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record.
9:15am
Cause called for Small claims appeal. Zachary Smith, Esq. appears for Respondent Zi Qiang Chen; Rebecca Tovshteyn, appellant, appears in Pro Per. Also appearing is Mable Cheung, Mandarin interpreter, for Mr. Chen. May Chen, also present, was the driver of the car in question, and is the daughter of Mr. Chen. Witnesses are sworn. (Judge Myles will be addressing the cross-complaint as well as the Small Claims Appeal action.)

Mr. Smith questions May Cheung, driver of the car. He also has some questions for Mr. Chen. Ms. Tovshteyn then presents her case in the narrative. Judge Myles has questions for the parties. Mr. Smith then questions respondents again. Judge Myles has questions.
9:37am
Matter is submitted. No new evidence is admitted or retained. (Appellant's Small Claims evidence is returned to her in open court by Judge Myles.)
